title: Week 6: Rohan Pushes Mini dApp to Real Users
paragraphs: Rohan is a self-taught full-stack builder who prioritizes practical utility over flashy prototypes. By week six, he shifted his focus from demo-ready to user-ready and started onboarding real users to his mini dApp.

His target project solves freelance milestone payments where release conditions are transparent and easy to verify. The core goal is reducing ambiguity in small team collaborations where payment expectations often live in scattered messages.

He chose this problem because he had experienced delayed payments as a freelancer and understood how quickly trust can break. For him, this is a trust design challenge as much as a smart contract challenge.

Rohan uses a strict execution loop: contract-first modeling, edge-case testing, and Friday user validation. He balances this with active client work and typically builds before morning calls. That consistency helped him ship a stable build and prepare for Demo Day quality review.
